title: Sum of Digits
prompt_md:
## Goal

Compute the sum of the decimal digits of a non-negative integer **using recursion** -- no loops anywhere in your digit logic. Decide for yourself what stops the recursion and how each call shrinks the problem.

## Input

- A single non-negative integer `n` (0 <= n <= 1000000000).

## Output

- The sum of the digits of `n`, on one line.

title: Recursive Power
prompt_md:
## Goal

Compute `base` raised to the power `exp` **using recursion** -- no loops. You choose how to break the problem down; just make sure every path reaches a stopping point and the exponent moves toward it on each call.

## Input

- Two integers on one line: `base` (-10 <= base <= 10) and `exp` (0 <= exp <= 30).

## Output

- The value of `base` raised to `exp`, on one line. (The result fits in a 64-bit integer.)
